key_rules: Safety e-Report is the primary official domain frame for the MVP.
The official domain groups include safety reports, illegal parking, vehicle/traffic violations, and life-inconvenience reports.
Use this card to map user issues to the first official-domain layer before applying DongneSOS privacy profiles.
evidence_requirements: Use the issue-specific source cards for precise evidence requirements.
If no precise card exists, ask for factual location, observed time, issue description, and photo availability.
privacy_notes: Do not expose reporter or bystander PII in public copy.
Use official-only lane for target identifiers when the official domain requires them.
routing_notes: If the exact local agency differs by district, return needs_official_verification rather than pretending to know the final department.
limitations: This overview provides category structure, not every local processing rule.

key_rules: Normal safety/life-inconvenience reporting flow expects report type, photo or video attachment, location selection, and report content.
The service should guide the user to gather evidence before leaving the scene.
evidence_requirements: issue type
photo or video when available
location selected by GPS, map, or address
short factual report content
privacy_notes: Photo guidance must distinguish official evidence from public sharing.
Unrelated faces, private unit numbers, phone numbers, delivery labels, and home interiors should not be exposed publicly.
routing_notes: Use as a general evidence-readiness card when the precise local guide is not available.
limitations: Local government processing details can differ.

cards.source_name: 안전신문고 불법 주정차 신고 기본 요건
key_rules: Illegal-parking reports can require multiple photos from the same position/direction with enough time gap.
Target vehicle, surrounding background, location, and shooting time are evidence context, not merely text noise.
evidence_requirements: two or more photos
same or similar position and direction
at least one minute between photos for common illegal-parking zones
target vehicle and surrounding background identifiable
vehicle plate, location, and shooting time visible or inferable
privacy_notes: Target vehicle plate may be official-only evidence.
Vehicle plate should be removed from public neighbor-share text.
Bystander faces and children in photos should not be publicly shared.
limitations: Time gaps and accepted zones can differ by local category.

key_rules: 서울 guide treats some traffic-law violations as citizen-reportable through smartphone app channels.
Parking/stopping violation evidence should identify violation area, vehicle number, and shooting time.
Dedicated-lane violations can use photo or video depending on category.
evidence_requirements: same position and angle photos with time interval for parking/stopping violations
violation area identifiable
vehicle number identifiable
shooting time visible
submit within the guide's reporting window when applicable
privacy_notes: Vehicle number is official evidence for the target vehicle.
Do not put vehicle number in public neighbor-share text.
routing_notes: For Seoul-specific prompts, mention 서울스마트불편신고 or 안전신문고 as possible channels while still asking the user to verify.
limitations: This is Seoul-specific and should not be generalized to every district.

key_rules: Resident illegal-parking reports can lead to processing without field dispatch when evidence meets requirements.
Photos should identify violation area, vehicle number, and shooting time.
Some school-zone cases require the photo to show that it is a school zone.
evidence_requirements: two or more photos with same background and one-minute interval
violation area identifiable
vehicle number identifiable
shooting time shown
school-zone indication when reporting a school-zone case
privacy_notes: Vehicle plate can be official-only target evidence.
Public copy should not expose vehicle plate or children in school-zone photos.
routing_notes: Use as an example of local variation and remind the user to check the relevant district guide.
limitations: District-specific content may vary by city or ward.

cards.source_name: 창원시 불법 주정차 주민신고제 입증사진 요건
key_rules: Some local guides require photos taken through the Safety e-Report app for illegal-parking resident reports.
The guide distinguishes one-minute and five-minute intervals by zone type.
Background context helps identify violation location.
evidence_requirements: photos through Safety e-Report app when the local guide requires it
two or more photos of the same target vehicle from same location and direction
one-minute interval for major illegal-parking zones
five-minute interval for other zones when applicable
surrounding building or signboard context for location
vehicle number and shooting time shown
privacy_notes: Target plate is official-only evidence.
Public sharing should hide plate and unrelated faces.
routing_notes: Use this card to warn that locally valid photo rules can be stricter than a generic summary.
limitations: This is a local guide and should be used as a local-specific or cautionary source card.

key_rules: Use 국민신문고 as a fallback civil-petition frame when Safety e-Report routing is uncertain.
Public materials distinguish inquiry/interpretation requests, system-improvement proposals, and grievance petitions.
evidence_requirements: factual background
question or requested action
related agency or local context when known
attachments only when relevant
privacy_notes: Do not put unnecessary reporter PII in draft body.
Use official form fields for required reporter identity or contact information.
routing_notes: If the user asks where to ask or how to phrase an administrative inquiry, produce a neutral 관할 확인 요청 draft.
limitations: This is not the right primary route for emergency, traffic enforcement, or issues with a clearer specialized channel.

key_rules: Grievance-petition routing is appropriate when the issue is about administrative action or inaction rather than an incident report.
ACRC routes online grievance applications through 국민신문고.
evidence_requirements: what administrative action or inaction caused the burden
agency or office involved when known
dates, prior requests, replies, or case numbers when available
privacy_notes: Avoid public neighbor-share text for disputes with agencies or named officials.
Use factual chronology and remove personal attacks.
routing_notes: Do not route ordinary illegal parking, emergency, or simple civic-facility defects here unless the user is complaining about agency handling.
limitations: Exact 처리기간 and scope can vary by 민원 type and agency.

key_rules: Vehicle registration number is generally not personal information by itself.
It can become personal information in special contexts when combined with other information or used to identify an owner/driver.
DongneSOS should treat target vehicle plate as official-only when needed and redact it from public sharing.
evidence_requirements: when vehicle is the report target, keep plate available for official report lane
when vehicle is unrelated, treat as bystander identifier and redact
privacy_notes: Do not publish vehicle plate in neighbor-share text.
Be stricter when plate is combined with name, phone, apartment resident DB, unit, or location history.
routing_notes: Use this card whenever the user asks whether a plate can appear in official/public text.
limitations: This card is product-risk guidance, not legal advice.

key_rules: Personal information collection/use depends on legal basis and purpose.
DongneSOS should not claim final legality; it should minimize data in generated text and separate official-only from public copy.
evidence_requirements: only include sensitive or identifying details when tied to the official reporting purpose
avoid repeating reporter PII in body text
privacy_notes: Use data minimization as a product principle.
When in doubt, keep sensitive values out of public text and place user-only guidance in private notes.
routing_notes: Use as a cross-cutting privacy basis, not an issue classifier.
limitations: High-stakes legal interpretation requires competent authority or counsel.

cards.source_name: 민원 처리에 관한 법률 제7조 정보 보호
key_rules: Administrative petition handling has confidentiality and purpose-limitation duties for petition contents and personal information of petitioners or specific persons.
DongneSOS should reflect that official processing and public sharing are different lanes.
evidence_requirements: official report can contain necessary facts for processing
public neighbor-share copy should remove personal identifiers and sensitive specifics
privacy_notes: Do not conflate official submission content with public community posting.
Use public copy as a notice, not a duplicate of the official report.
routing_notes: Use as a policy justification for privacy lanes.
limitations: This card does not decide which exact agency receives the petition.

key_rules: 국민신문고 processes personal data for petition, proposal, and report handling by competent agencies and linked systems.
DongneSOS should not include reporter identity/contact in body text when the official form has separate fields.
evidence_requirements: use separate official form fields for user identity/contact where required
keep draft body focused on facts and request
privacy_notes: Public sharing should not copy official petition details that include specific persons.
Private notes can remind the user that official forms may ask for identity/contact data.
routing_notes: Use when the user asks whether official channels process personal data.
limitations: Exact collection items can differ by petition type and competent agency.

key_rules: ACRC privacy notice frames personal-data handling around public service, petition handling, and official duties.
DongneSOS should keep generated text minimal and purpose-bound.
evidence_requirements: facts necessary for petition handling
avoid unnecessary sensitive personal details
privacy_notes: Use minimum necessary detail in official drafts.
Use stricter redaction for public sharing.
routing_notes: Use as a supporting privacy source for 국민신문고 and ACRC-related petition flows.
limitations: This source is privacy-policy context, not a category-specific reporting guide.
